introduction: Series Completion in verbal/logical aptitude asks you to find the next term, spot the wrong term, or recognize the rule in letter, number, or mixed series.
explanation:
CONCEPT

A series follows a hidden rule: constant difference, increasing difference, geometric ratio, letter skips, alternating patterns, or combined letter–number codes. Your task is to discover and apply that rule.

RULES

• One rule should explain every term.
• Wrong-number questions: exactly one term breaks the rule.
• Letter series use A=1 … Z=26 positions.
• Mixed series may change letters and numbers on separate tracks.

TRICKS / SHORTCUT TECHNIQUES

• Write differences between consecutive terms.
• Split odd and even positions if the series looks irregular.
• Check squares, cubes, and primes for number series.
• For letters, convert to positions immediately when stuck.

STEP-BY-STEP SOLVING METHOD

1) Observe the given terms.
2) Test simple arithmetic/letter skips.
3) Try second-order differences or two interleaved series.
4) Predict the asked term or identify the odd term.
5) Verify on all given terms.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Stopping after matching the first two gaps.
2) Off-by-one letter counting.
3) Forcing a complex rule when a simple one works.
4) Missing an alternating pattern.
5) Arithmetic slips on growing differences.

formulas.title: Growing difference
formulas.content: Differences increase by a fixed k each step

formulas.title: Letter skip
formulas.content: Next letter = current ± n positions in alphabet

formulas.title: Alternating series
formulas.content: Odd terms rule A; even terms rule B

shortcutTips.title: Difference line
shortcutTips.tip: Always jot consecutive differences before guessing.

shortcutTips.title: Split tracks
shortcutTips.tip: If messy, separate odd-position and even-position terms.

shortcutTips.title: Position numbers
shortcutTips.tip: Map letters to 1–26 for skip patterns.

shortcutTips.title: Verify fully
shortcutTips.tip: A rule that fails on one term is wrong.

solvedExamples.problem: Find next letters: A, C, F, J, ?
